organization: Wearables and Biointegrated Technologies Lab · University of Calgary
summary: Developing a fluorescence and colorimetric lateral flow assay (LFA) reader and an accessible open-source quantification app.
responsibilities: Building a fluorescence and colorimetric LFA reader alongside an open-source LFA quantification app in Python and R, available on mobile.
Designing an accessible platform that rivals existing LFA systems costing around $10,000.
Developing LFAs and validating results using gel electrophoresis and lab experiments.

summary: Building physiotherapy modules that are remote, accessible, and gamified for at-home rehab.
responsibilities: Developing hardware modules that help patients complete physiotherapy at home with a more engaging, game-like experience.
Working with ESP32s, custom-made flex sensors, circuitry, electronics, CAD design, and glove fabrication.
Focusing on making rehab tools more portable and accessible outside clinical settings.

summary: Postpartum support for mothers — building the app, product vision, and investor conversations.
responsibilities: Developed a mobile app to help new mothers screen for postpartum depression and connect with healthcare and support resources.
Programmed the app and designed clinician-facing data tools using validated assessments such as the Edinburgh Postnatal Depression Scale (EPDS).
Pitched to investors and worked with advisors and mentors to collaborate on the company's vision.

role: Mechanical Design & Programming Mentor · Outreach Lead · Drive Coach
summary: Mentoring, outreach, and competition leadership across Calgary robotics teams.
responsibilities: Led FIRST LEGO League workshops in the community, teaching youth to build and program with LEGO Spike.
Co-founded an all-girls robotics team, mentoring in mechanical design and programming.
Served as drive coach and strategy lead during competitions.
Built skills in Fusion 360, SolidWorks, Java, 3D printing, CNC, and laser cutting.
Featured on CTV News and by FIRST Robotics for community impact.

summary: Guided children aged 8–12 through coding workshops and math practice sessions.
responsibilities: Explained fundamental programming concepts in an engaging, age-appropriate way.
Supported students in developing problem-solving and critical thinking skills through hands-on activities.
Assisted with lesson setup, activity coordination, and building confidence across skill levels.

organization: Intelligent Dynamics Control Lab · University of Calgary
summary: Designed an IoT-based bicopter to monitor crop temperature, humidity, and light levels.
responsibilities: Integrated real-time sensor data to support irrigation optimization and water efficiency.
Collaborated with the research team to prototype autonomous agricultural monitoring systems.
Awarded the UCalgary WISE Research Award for research excellence.

organization: Vascular Training Platform (VAST) · University of Calgary
summary: Studied cerebral blood flow and brain health through neuroimaging and data analysis.
responsibilities: Conducted MRI and Transcranial Doppler analysis to study cerebral blood flow and brain health.
Analyzed dementia-related datasets using R to identify cognitive and lifestyle trends.
Supported cognitive testing and data collection for CAN-Protect research studies.

summary: Ranked Top 10 in Calgary (2024) at the provincial level.
responsibilities: Developed strong public speaking, argumentation, and critical thinking skills.
Competed in policy-based debates requiring rapid analysis and persuasion.

summary: Represented 1000+ students in Alberta, working with the Minister of Education and council members on education system issues.
responsibilities: One of 40 students selected province-wide to provide youth perspectives on provincial education.
Focused on enhancing the COVID re-entry plan for students, particularly K–6.
Proposed a transition period dedicated to reading and math while leveraging STEAM courses.

summary: Contributed 100+ hours to community service projects across Calgary.
responsibilities: Supported youth, seniors, and vulnerable populations through social, environmental, and cultural initiatives.
Demonstrated leadership, teamwork, and initiative in collaborative community projects.

summary: Shadowed Vincent Chaley, an investor in prime energy, meeting with startups and learning about entrepreneurship.
responsibilities: Attended the 4th seminar of CDL Rockies where startups presented to mentors and sponsors.
Gained insight at the intersection of commerce and agriculture and expanded my professional network.
